euler_problems: Problem 587 - Concave Triangle: Find the least n for which the concave triangle occupies less than 0.1% of the L-section formed by a square drawn around circles.
explanation: The code defines a program that calculates the area between a circular section and lines that intersect it. It uses mathematical functions to find points of intersection and numerically compute the area under the curve in relation to the circle's radius. The main function iteratively increases a parameter 'n' that represents the slope of the lines and measures how the area under these lines compares to the total area of the circle until a specific precision is achieved.
name: 2017-02-17_project-euler-587
subject: project euler 587
summary: This project calculates the area between a circle and a series of lines, solving a problem from Project Euler.
